title: Outbox drain — cloud routine wired as durable backstop
summary: The Slack outbox now has a cloud-side hourly drain (trig_01KDtE6uhFhBTnV3s5T3mwWx, every hour at :15 past) on top of the existing local Stop-hook drain. Every research publish reaches #poc-stratum within an hour even when no Claude Code session is open. The routine also scans recent commits for publish-class artifacts that lack outbox entries and posts a violation alert if it finds one — closing the discipline gap that caused three earlier publishes today to drain only because the Chairman caught it. Eglin's profile updated in commit 2541df0 to make the outbox-entry rule non-overridable by parent-session instructions. Pre-commit guard scripts staged but unwired pending Chairman approval. This outbox entry itself is the first end-to-end test of the new routine.
